step_by_step_thinking:
Shyam presents with gross hematuria and 80% dysmorphic RBCs following an upper respiratory tract infection. This pattern suggests IgA nephropathy, where post-infectious glomerulonephritis often leads to hematuria due to increased deposition of circulating immune complexes involving IgA antibodies. Therefore, the best fit among the given options would be:
\boxed{B} Based on the history of recent upper respiratory tract infection followed by hematuria with predominantly dysmorphic red blood cells, the likely diagnosis aligning well with the evidence supporting post-streptococcal glomerulonephritis (PSGN) or similar patterns seen in IgA nephropathy is
